title: agent-usage/v2 — granular breakdown of resources an agent consumed producing a change
description: Additive to agent-usage/v1 (a separate predicate type, not a superset field bolted onto v1's closed schema). Same self-reported, unverifiable-until-signing caveats as v1. Adds five breakdown dimensions — by_model, by_thread, by_phase, by_tool, turn_shape — derived from the same harness session transcript v1 reads, for the same window.

source.description: Where the numbers come from, e.g. 'claude-code session transcript'.

by_model.description: Tokens keyed by message.model — distinguishes main-thread and subagent models (e.g. claude-sonnet-5 vs claude-fable-5).

by_thread.description: Tokens split by the transcript's isSidechain flag: main thread vs offloaded subagent work.

by_phase.description: Tokens split at the first implementation commit observable in git after the change opened: exploration_and_implementation precedes it, governance_overhead (evidence pinning, PR description, etc.) follows it.

boundary_commit.description: Git commit SHA of the first implementation commit; the phase boundary.

by_tool.description: cache_creation tokens attributed to each tool name, by whose tool_result content was ingested into context that turn. Approximate: when a turn batches multiple tool calls, their combined cache_creation is split proportionally by each tool's result size.

turn_shape.description: Shape of the conversation: how many turns, how large context grew, and cache-lifetime split.

peak_context_tokens.description: Max of (input + cache_read) across any single request in the window.

mean_marginal_work_tokens.description: Mean of (input + output + cache_creation) per turn.
